We're inclusive. We listen and build our movement together. And we rely on grassroots support to power this campaign -- people just like you.

It's a far cry from Republicans and their brand of politics. Theirs is a politics of division. It's a brand of politics that relies on big money from groups like the Koch-brothers funded Republican Governors Association. Their politics means running TV ads before starting the tough, important work of grassroots organizing.

With ballots in the mailboxes of voters across Washington, our grassroots campaign is mobilizing to get out the vote -- right now. We'll rely on this grassroots support to go toe-to-toe with Republicans and their big money groups. From grassroots organizing to grassroots fundraising our campaign is about building power from the ground up.

[**Help grow our grassroots! name: Russ Feingold
subject: Uniquely Wisconsin
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
Wisconsinites like to do things differently. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4382/1c469400/60a75f9/356e71d/3972679625/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-jul?utm_source=ema_2016-07-16_jul-fec-ema1_nd&refcode=ema_2016-07-16_jul-fec-ema1_nd&utm_campaign=jul-fec-ema1&utm_medium=ema") Friend -- I talk a lot about things that are uniquely Wisconsin. From our political progressivism to our community-owned Green Bay Packers -- even the way we make an old-fashioned -- Wisconsinites like to do things differently. Here's another thing that's unique to Wisconsin: Our next FEC fundraising deadline is July 20\. That's right, because of the primary, we'll be filing our next report earlier than every other Senate campaign. Because of your support, we've reported some amazing fundraising numbers so far. (Thank you!) Now, we have a unique opportunity to show our grassroots strength even earlier. I'm setting a grassroots fundraising goal of 7,500 individual contributions between now and the July 20 deadline. Will you join us? **[Will you contribute today to help us reach 7,500 contributions by midnight on July 20?](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4382/1c469400/60a75f9/356e71d/3972679625/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-jul?utm_source=ema_2016-07-16_jul-fec-ema1_nd&refcode=ema_2016-07-16_jul-fec-ema1_nd&utm_campaign=jul-fec-ema1&utm_medium=ema")** [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4382/1c469400/60a75f9/356e71d/3972679625/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-jul?utm_source=ema_2016-07-16_jul-fec-ema1_nd&refcode=ema_2016-07-16_jul-fec-ema1_nd&utm_campaign=jul-fec-ema1&utm_medium=ema") [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4382/1c469400/60a75f9/356e71d/3972679625/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-jul?utm_source=ema_2016-07-16_jul-fec-ema1_nd&refcode=ema_2016-07-16_jul-fec-ema1_nd&utm_campaign=jul-fec-ema1&utm_medium=ema") _Or, if your payment information is saved with ActBlue, click below and your contribution will go through immediately:_ | | [$5 »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4382/1c469400/60a75f9/356e71a/3972679625/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-jul?utm_source=ema_2016-07-16_jul-fec-ema1_nd&refcode=ema_2016-07-16_jul-fec-ema1_nd&utm_campaign=jul-fec-ema1&utm_medium=ema&express_lane=true&amount=5")

I don't have to tell you that all eyes are on Wisconsin right now, especially from the right-wing special interest groups. Six years ago, they fought hard to buy the Wisconsin Senate seat, and Senator Ron Johnson has become their model legislator. They aren't going to give that up easily.
But we have a tremendous opportunity to show our strength. And, of course, every dollar raised will be put to use immediately, ensuring we have every resource necessary to win in November.
With your help, I'm confident that we can hit our uniquely Wisconsin July 20 goal of 7,500 individual contributions. Thank you for being a part of our grassroots campaign.
Sincerely,
Russ Feingold

Friend, We need a North Carolina that works for everyone. It's what Roy has fought for as attorney general, and it's what he will fight for as governor. Governor McCrory's political gamesmanship has cost the state hundreds of jobs and millions of dollars. That's why Roy announced his plan to create jobs for people across North Carolina. **[If you're with Roy, will you add your name to let him know you've got his back?](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120261e/2fde7c66/400c7034/156a7518/2752430357/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/stand-with-roy-on-jobs?utm_campaign=adv_jobs3_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em16_160716_adv_gq_jobs3_ful__tn&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")** Here are just a few of Roy's ideas from the plan:
* Investments in education are crucial for building a strong workforce. He will work to increase access to the education and training necessary for skilled labor.
* A plan to address NC's aging infrastructure is one of our greatest needs. We can solve this problem and put people to work by rebuilding our infrastructure.
* He will commit to small businesses by working to ensure they have the support and access to capital they need.

We released the full report online, so you can see Roy's plan for yourself.
| | [**Stand with Roy ->**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120261e/2fde7c66/400c7034/156a7518/2752430357/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/stand-with-roy-on-jobs?utm_campaign=adv_jobs3_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em16_160716_adv_gq_jobs3_ful__tn&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

Friend,

I am honored to join Donald Trump as his running mate in the fight to Make America Great Again!

subject: Trump can barely pick a vice president
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Two is worse than one | Friend --

Donald Trump has been running a campaign predicated on his strength as a decision maker -- he keeps touting his judgment, his business savvy, and his self-confidence.

How did that play out when it came time to pick his running mate?

The entire process was public and unapologetically competitive. It felt like watching a reality show: Trump put out a short list, went back and forth on his decision, pushed the deadline to decide, pulled down his own press conference, then upstaged himself at the last minute by announcing on Twitter.

And just after he told the world that Mike Pence would be his vice presidential nominee, CNN reported that he was up past midnight on Thursday evening trying to find a way out of his decision.

That's Donald Trump as a leader. Divisive, sure. Decisive? No.

The kinds of decisions that come across the president’s desk are not easy ones. We need a person in the Oval Office who we trust to have the final say.

name: Jay Inslee
subject: A future we can be proud of
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
What would past leaders do with the climate crisis? [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTIwNDc5OAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYxMTM5MzA1S0&mt=1&rt=0) | | _**" We have fallen heirs to the most glorious heritage a people ever received, and each one must do his part if we wish to show that the nation is worthy of its good fortune."**_
---
Dear Friend,
That is one of my favorite quotes from a man of many words and legendary importance to our country, President Theodore Roosevelt.
I often think of President Roosevelt's bold leadership in conserving some of our nation's most beautiful and wild places. And I think of generation after generation of American leaders who saw a crisis and took action.
That's the kind of leadership we need when it comes to the climate crisis. I know that bold leaders like President Roosevelt wouldn't take no for an answer when it comes to acting on one of the biggest moral issues of our time. Because this is what it comes down to: **Acting now to protect our planet is the right thing to do.**
[**Do you agree? Please, sign on today in support of climate action.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=OTIwNDc5OAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYxMTM5MzA1S0&mt=1&rt=0)
[](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTIwNDc5OAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYxMTM5MzA1S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Every day we wake up to more news about the threat of climate change: Islands disappearing and weather becoming more unpredictable. You and I share a bold vision for action with Washington leading the United States and the world toward a clean energy future. It's one of the reasons that fighting with everything we've got this election year is so important, because continuing the clean energy movement for four more years in Washington must be part of our agenda.
I'm proud of what we've already accomplished, from developing a clean energy fund that is investing millions here in Washington state, to introducing an important clean air rule that will put a cap on carbon for our biggest polluters. It's like Teddy Roosevelt said, "each one must do his part." So our bold work must continue.
[**Join me today and add your voice: We must take bold action to combat climate change, and the work must start -- and continue -- in Washington state.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=OTIwNDc5OAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYxMTM5MzA1S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you for your support today.
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee
[**ADD YOUR NAME »**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=OTIwNDc5OAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYxMTM5MzA1S0&mt=1&rt=0)

802-595-3776

**Phil Scott: State Revenues Show Unsustainable Spending; Growing Crisis of Affordability**
_Highlights plans to contain spending and prioritize the economy _
Montpelier, Vt. -- Phil Scott today issued the following statement on state revenue results, budgeting and addressing Vermont's crisis of affordability:

"On Friday the Shumlin administration announced that General Fund revenues grew by only 2.3% last fiscal year, an increase of $32 million over the prior year[1]. If not for the $30 million in new taxes enacted by the legislature in 2015, revenue growth would have been essentially zero[2]. Clearly we have not been budgeting sustainably. Not a day goes by without one of the Democratic candidates proposing new taxes or higher spending; and not a day goes by without Vermonters telling me they are struggling under the growing crisis of affordability.

"I firmly believe we cannot raise more taxes – we do not have that capacity. That’s why my administration will focus on growing the economy and I will not propose or sign a budget that grows faster than the economy or wages in the previous year. See the full details of my proposal to establish truth in budgeting and real fiscal responsibility _[click here](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=c5468dc922&e=19cebfe173)_.

"Today’s announcement reminds us that our tax base is shrinking. The solution to the revenue shortfall is not new ways to tax people – like through fees and surcharges – it is through growing the economy organically and making state government more efficient. I have laid out my plan to modernize state government _[here](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=db5d3e1d30&e=19cebfe173). _We must stop limiting growth and economic success by raising taxes and encouraging red tape.

"The best way to balance the state budget is by growing the economy and reforming state government, not raising taxes."
* * *
[1] http://aoa.vermont.gov/sites/aoa/files/revenue-economy/RevenueRpts/June%202016%20Press%20Release.pdf [2] http://www.leg.state.vt.us/jfo/appropriations/fy_2016/conference_summary.pdf ###

clean_body: | | | | Media Contact: Ethan Latour mailto:name@philscott.org 802-595-3776 Phil Scott: State Revenues Show Unsustainable Spending; Growing Crisis of Affordability Highlights plans to contain spending and prioritize the economy Montpelier, Vt. -- Phil Scott today issued the following statement on state revenue results, budgeting and addressing Vermont's crisis of affordability: "On Friday the Shumlin administration announced that General Fund revenues grew by only 2.3% last fiscal year, an increase of $32 million over the prior year[1]. If not for the $30 million in new taxes enacted by the legislature in 2015, revenue growth would have been essentially zero[2]. Clearly we have not been budgeting sustainably. Not a day goes by without one of the Democratic candidates proposing new taxes or higher spending; and not a day goes by without Vermonters telling me they are struggling under the growing crisis of affordability. "I firmly believe we cannot raise more taxes – we do not have that capacity. That’s why my administration will focus on growing the economy and I will not propose or sign a budget that grows faster than the economy or wages in the previous year. See the full details of my proposal to establish truth in budgeting and real fiscal responsibility . "Today’s announcement reminds us that our tax base is shrinking. The solution to the revenue shortfall is not new ways to tax people – like through fees and surcharges – it is through growing the economy organically and making state government more efficient. I have laid out my plan to modernize state government . We must stop limiting growth and economic success by raising taxes and encouraging red tape. "The best way to balance the state budget is by growing the economy and reforming state government, not raising taxes." [1] [2] ### Paid for by Phil Scott For Vermont 2016** 46 Three Mile Bridge Rd. subject: one thing is absolutely clear about this Pence pick
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Trump and Pence could well be the most extreme right-wing ticket in modern history | Friend --

Trump’s new running mate, Mike Pence, has an atrocious record of anti-woman, homophobic, extreme conservative governance.

He also has some absolutely absurd ideas that make him a natural partner for the Trump ticket. To wit:

* **Mike Pence doesn’t believe smoking causes deaths.** He wrote on his website, “smoking doesn't kill. In fact, two out of every three smokers does not die from a smoking related illness.” ... last time I checked, that means one out of every three smokers DOES die from a smoking-related illness, right?
* **Mike Pence thinks climate change is a hoax.** Again, in his own words: "Global warming is a myth. The global warming treaty is a disaster. There, I said it."
* **Mike Pence shut down the only HIV testing center in Scott County, Indiana -- in the name of preventing HIV/AIDS.** In a huge surprise, that county later experienced what the press called “an exploding HIV outbreak.”

Mike Pence might not think smoking, climate change, or HIV pose serious threats -- but one thing is absolutely clear:

Mike Pence himself is a pretty serious threat to the American people.

Between the two of them, Trump and Pence could well be the most extreme right-wing ticket in the modern history of American politics -- and they’re just days away from accepting the Republican nominations. **Before they spend a week in Cleveland attacking Hillary (with network TV coverage every night), we have to show the world that we’re ready to take them on. Commit to volunteer right now, and let’s get to work:**

Christina
